### Description

*Linum perenne* L., known commonly as blue flax or perennial flax (berglin in Swedish), was first described by Carl Linnaeus in Species Plantarum (1753, p. 277). It is a member of the *Linum perenne* group, a collection of several species widely distributed across Europe, Asia and North America. Most representatives of this group, including *L. perenne* L. itself, are concentrated in Central Europe and exhibit heterostyly (Ockendon, 1968). This specialized reproductive system ensures that fertilization occurs only between different floral morphs, effectively preventing self-pollination (Murray, 1986). Recent genomic research by Zervakis, Postel et al. (2025) identified the 3.8 Mb S-locus supergene governing heterostyly. The two alleles at the S-locus differ in length, with the longer and dominant allele being specific to and hemizygous in short-styled individuals. This unique structural arrangement ensures the stable inheritance of distinct "pin" and "thrum" floral architectures across generations, maintaining the balanced polymorphism required for cross-pollination in wild populations.

#### How to cite

If you use the data presented in the genome portal from this species in your research, please cite the original publication:

```{style=citation}
Zervakis, P.-I., Postel, Z., Losvik, A., Fracassetti, M., Solér, L., Proux-Wéra, E., Bunikis, I., Churcher, A., & Slotte, T. (2025). Genomic studies in *Linum* shed light on the evolution of the distyly supergene and the molecular basis of convergent floral evolution. New Phytologist, 247(6), 2964--2981. https://doi.org/10.1111/nph.70392
```

If you have used the pages for this species in the Genome Portal, please refer to it in-text as: "The *Linum perenne* entry in the Swedish Reference Genome Portal (Retrieved <span class="todays-date"></span>)." and use the following for the bibliography:

```{style=citation}
Swedish Reference Genome Portal (Retrieved DATE_ACCESSED), SciLifeLab Data Centre, version VERSION_NUMBER from <https://genomes.scilifelab.se>, [RRID:SCR_026008](https://rrid.site/data/record/nlx_144509-1/SCR_026008/resolver?q=rrid:scr_026008)
```

#### References

- Linnaeus, C. (1753). Species Plantarum: Exhibentes plantas rite cognitas, ad genera relatas, cum differentiis specificis, nominibus trivialibus, synonymis selectis, locis natalibus, secundum systema sexuale digestas. Laurentii Salvii. <https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/bibliography/669>
- Murray, B. G. (1986). Floral Biology and Self-Incompatibility in *Linum*. Botanical Gazette, 147(3), 327--333. <https://doi.org/10.1086/337599>
- Ockendon, D. J. (1968). Biosystematic Studies in the *Linum perenne* Group. New Phytologist, 67(4), 787--813. <https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1469-8137.1968.tb06396.x>
- Zervakis, P.-I., Postel, Z., Losvik, A., Fracassetti, M., Solér, L., Proux-Wéra, E., Bunikis, I., Churcher, A., & Slotte, T. (2025). Genomic studies in *Linum* shed light on the evolution of the distyly supergene and the molecular basis of convergent floral evolution. New Phytologist, 247(6), 2964--2981. <https://doi.org/10.1111/nph.70392>
